message:
feat(util): add DryRun helper infrastructure (rust_proxy-ar9)

Add DryRun struct for consistent dry-run messaging across commands:

- DryRun::new(enabled) / DryRun::from(bool) / DryRun::default()
- would_do(action) - prints "Would: <action>" and returns true to skip
- would_do_fmt(format_args!(...)) - same with format string support
- execute_or_skip(action, op, default) - conditional execution helper
- is_enabled() - check if dry-run is active

This infrastructure unblocks rust_proxy-btn (stop command dry-run) and
rust_proxy-hvv (service commands dry-run).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"rust_proxy-99b","title":"Implement service file template with placeholders","description":"## Scope

Implement the systemd service file template with configurable placeholders.

## Implementation Details

```rust
fn generate_service_file(
    binary_path: &Path,
    config_path: &Path,
    user: &str,
    group: &str,
    hardened: bool,
) -> String {
    let mut service = format!(r#"[Unit]
Description=Rust Proxy - High-performance HTTPS proxy
Documentation=https://github.com/yourorg/rust_proxy
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart={binary} start --foreground --config {config}
ExecReload=/bin/kill -HUP $MAINPID
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5
TimeoutStopSec=30

User={user}
Group={group}

# Logging
StandardOutput=journal
StandardError=journal
SyslogIdentifier=rp

# Resource limits
LimitNOFILE=65535

"#,
        binary = binary_path.display(),
        config = config_path.display(),
        user = user,
        group = group,
    );

    if hardened {
        service.push_str(&generate_hardening_section());
    }

    service.push_str(r#"
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
"#);

    service
}
```

### Template Variables

- `{binary}` - Full path to rp binary
- `{config}` - Path to config file
- `{user}` - Service user
- `{group}` - Service group

## Acceptance Criteria

- Valid systemd unit file syntax
- All placeholders replaced correctly
- Works with systemd-analyze verify","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T14:09:36.864929167-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T14:09:36.864929167-05:00"}

 {"id":"rust_proxy-b2v","title":"Integrate rich errors throughout codebase","description":"## Integrate Rich Errors Throughout Codebase

### Overview

Replace generic error messages throughout the codebase with rich error types that include context and suggestions. This is the integration work to make the error system actually useful.

### Files to Update

1. **src/proxy.rs** - Connection errors
2. **src/health.rs** - Health check failures
3. **src/config.rs** - Config parsing/validation
4. **src/daemon.rs** - Startup failures
5. **src/commands/*.rs** - CLI command errors

### Migration Pattern

**Before:**
```rust
TcpStream::connect(&proxy_addr).await?;
// Generic "connection refused" error
```

**After:**
```rust
TcpStream::connect(&proxy_addr).await
    .map_err(|e| ProxyError::ConnectionFailed {
        proxy_id: proxy.id.clone(),
        address: proxy_addr.clone(),
        source: e,
    })?;
// Rich error with proxy context
```

### Integration Checklist

**Proxy Connection (src/proxy.rs):**
- [ ] Wrap connection errors with ProxyError::ConnectionFailed
- [ ] Wrap timeout errors with ProxyError::Timeout
- [ ] Wrap TLS errors with ProxyError::TlsError
- [ ] Include proxy_id in all errors

**Health Check (src/health.rs):**
- [ ] Wrap check failures with HealthCheckError
- [ ] Include target information
- [ ] Include latency on timeout

**Config (src/config.rs):**
- [ ] Wrap TOML parse errors with line/column
- [ ] Wrap validation errors with field information
- [ ] Include file path in all errors

**Daemon (src/daemon.rs):**
- [ ] Wrap bind failures with DaemonError::PortInUse
- [ ] Wrap PID file errors with DaemonError::PidFile
- [ ] Include relevant paths/ports

**CLI Commands:**
- [ ] Use format_error_with_suggestions for all user-facing errors
- [ ] Ensure JSON output includes error structure
- [ ] Exit codes match error severity

### Error Display Implementation

```rust
// In main.rs or cli.rs
fn main() {
    if let Err(e) = run() {
        let formatted = if args.json {
            error_to_json(&*e)
        } else {
            format_error_with_suggestions(&*e)
        };
        eprintln!("{}", formatted);
        std::process::exit(1);
    }
}
```

### Testing the Integration

For each error path:
1. Trigger the error condition
2. Verify error message includes context
3. Verify suggestions are relevant
4. Verify JSON format is valid

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All proxy connection errors use ProxyError
- [ ] All config errors use ConfigError with location
- [ ] All daemon errors use DaemonError
- [ ] format_error_with_suggestions used for CLI output
- [ ] JSON output includes structured errors
- [ ] No generic "Error:" messages without context
- [ ] All errors have at least one suggestion","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T14:56:12.983528237-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T14:56:12.983528237-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_proxy-b2v","depends_on_id":"rust_proxy-x3z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T14:57:07.455789467-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rust_proxy-b5m","title":"Implement automatic proxy health checks with failover","description":"## Overview

Add automatic health monitoring for configured proxies with intelligent failover to healthy alternatives when the active proxy becomes unavailable. This is a significant reliability feature that makes rust_proxy suitable for production use.

## Background & Motivation

**Current Problem:**
- If active proxy becomes unreachable, connections fail silently or with cryptic errors
- Users must manually detect the issue (often via user complaints)
- Users must manually switch to another proxy via CLI
- No visibility into proxy health status
- Downtime continues until human intervention

**Solution Benefits:**
1. Continuous health monitoring of all proxies
2. Quick detection of unhealthy proxies (configurable threshold)
3. Automatic traffic rerouting to healthy alternatives
4. Clear visibility into health status via `rust_proxy status`
5. Reduced downtime from minutes/hours to seconds

## Critical Design Decisions

### 1. Runtime Active Proxy Management

**Problem:** Current architecture loads config once at startup. Failover requires changing active proxy at runtime.

**Solution:** Introduce `RuntimeState` separate from file-based Config:
```rust
pub struct RuntimeState {
    /// Currently active proxy (may differ from config.active_proxy during failover)
    pub effective_proxy: Arc<RwLock<Option<String>>>,
    /// Original active proxy (for failback)
    pub original_proxy: Option<String>,
    /// When failover occurred
    pub failover_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
}

// Daemon uses RuntimeState.effective_proxy instead of config.active_proxy
// Failover updates RuntimeState without touching config file
```

### 2. Health Check Method

**Problem:** Using external endpoints (httpbin.org) is unreliable and has privacy concerns.

**Solution:** Test proxy connectivity directly:
```rust
async fn health_check(proxy: &ProxyDef, timeout: Duration) -> HealthCheckResult {
    // 1. TCP connect to proxy host:port
    let stream = TcpStream::connect(&proxy.addr()).await?;
    
    // 2. Send CONNECT to a well-known, stable endpoint
    let request = format!(
        "CONNECT www.google.com:443 HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: www.google.com\r\n\r\n"
    );
    stream.write_all(request.as_bytes()).await?;
    
    // 3. Read response line
    let mut buf = [0u8; 1024];
    let n = stream.read(&mut buf).await?;
    let response = String::from_utf8_lossy(&buf[..n]);
    
    // 4. Check for success (200, 407 for auth, etc.)
    if response.starts_with("HTTP/1.1 200") || response.starts_with("HTTP/1.0 200") {
        Ok(HealthCheckResult::Healthy)
    } else if response.contains("407") {
        // Proxy requires auth - still "reachable" but may need credentials
        Ok(HealthCheckResult::AuthRequired)
    } else {
        Err(anyhow!("Unexpected response: {}", response.lines().next().unwrap_or("")))
    }
}
```

### 3. All Proxies Unhealthy Behavior

**Problem:** What happens when ALL proxies are unhealthy?

**Solution:** Configurable policy with safe default:
```toml
[settings]
# When all proxies unhealthy:
# - "use_last": keep using last proxy that was healthy (default, safest)
# - "fail_closed": reject new connections (explicit failure)
# - "round_robin": try proxies in rotation hoping one recovers
all_unhealthy_policy = "use_last"
```

Rationale for "use_last" default:
- Proxy might still work for some requests
- Better to try than to fail immediately
- User gets errors but routing continues
- Logging clearly indicates degraded state

### 4. Flapping Prevention

**Problem:** Unstable proxy rapidly toggles healthy/unhealthy, causing failover/failback churn.

**Solution:** Multi-level hysteresis:
```toml
[settings]
# Failures needed to mark unhealthy
consecutive_failures_threshold = 3

# Successes needed to mark healthy again
consecutive_successes_threshold = 2

# Minimum time between failovers (prevents rapid switching)
min_failover_interval_secs = 30

# Time to wait after recovery before failback
failback_delay_secs = 60

# Cooldown before retrying a failed proxy for failback
failed_proxy_cooldown_secs = 300
```

### 5. Startup Behavior

**Problem:** Should daemon start immediately or wait for health check?

**Solution:** Quick initial check with configurable blocking:
```toml
[settings]
# On startup:
# - "async": start immediately, check in background (default)
# - "blocking": wait for first health check before accepting (slower but safer)
startup_health_check = "async"

# If blocking, how long to wait for initial check
startup_health_timeout_secs = 10
```

## Configuration Schema

```toml
[settings]
# === Health Check Settings ===
# Master enable/disable
health_check_enabled = true

# How often to check each proxy
health_check_interval_secs = 30

# Timeout for individual health check
health_check_timeout_ms = 5000

# === Failure Detection ===
# Consecutive failures before marking unhealthy
consecutive_failures_threshold = 3

# Consecutive successes before marking healthy
consecutive_successes_threshold = 2

# === Failover Settings ===
# Automatically switch to healthy proxy
auto_failover = true

# What to do when all unhealthy: use_last | fail_closed | round_robin
all_unhealthy_policy = "use_last"

# Minimum seconds between failovers (flapping prevention)
min_failover_interval_secs = 30

# === Failback Settings ===
# Return to original proxy when it recovers
auto_failback = true

# Seconds to wait after recovery before failback
failback_delay_secs = 60

# Seconds before retrying a failed proxy for failback consideration
failed_proxy_cooldown_secs = 300

# === Startup ===
# Startup mode: async | blocking
startup_health_check = "async"

[[proxies]]
id = "mesh-us"
url = "http://us-wa.proxymesh.com:31280"
priority = 1  # Lower = higher priority for failover
# Optional: custom health check endpoint (defaults to proxy's own CONNECT)
# health_check_host = "www.google.com"
# health_check_port = 443
```

## State Schema

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ProxyStats {
    // Existing fields
    pub bytes_sent: u64,
    pub bytes_received: u64,
    pub last_active: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    pub activated_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    pub ping_avg_ms: Option<f64>,
    pub ping_samples: u64,
    pub last_ping_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    
    // New health fields
    pub health_status: HealthStatus,
    pub consecutive_failures: u32,
    pub consecutive_successes: u32,
    pub last_health_check: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    pub last_healthy: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    pub last_unhealthy: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    pub last_failure_reason: Option<String>,
    pub health_check_latency_ms: Option<f64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum HealthStatus {
    #[default]
    Unknown,
    Healthy,
    Unhealthy,
    // Note: Removed "Degraded" - too complex to define, keep it simple
}
```

## CLI Status Output

```
rust_proxy status

Active Proxy: mesh-us
  Status: ✓ Healthy
  Latency: 45ms (last check: 5s ago)

Daemon: running (pid 12345)
Rules: installed

Health Summary:
  ID        Status      Priority  Latency   Last Check  Streak
  mesh-us   ✓ Healthy   1         45ms      5s ago      5 ✓
  mesh-eu   ✓ Healthy   2         120ms     5s ago      3 ✓
  mesh-jp   ✗ Unhealthy 3         -         5s ago      3 ✗ (connection refused)

Health checks: enabled (interval: 30s, threshold: 3)
Auto-failover: enabled
Auto-failback: enabled (delay: 60s)
```

### Status During Failover
```
rust_proxy status

Active Proxy: mesh-eu (FAILOVER from mesh-us)
  Status: ✓ Healthy
  Latency: 120ms (last check: 5s ago)
  Failover at: 2025-01-18T12:00:00Z (2m ago)
  Original proxy: mesh-us (currently unhealthy)

...
```

## Logging

### Health Check Events
```
INFO  health: Health check passed proxy="mesh-us" latency_ms=45 streak=5
WARN  health: Health check failed proxy="mesh-jp" error="connection refused" failures=3
ERROR health: Proxy marked unhealthy proxy="mesh-jp" consecutive_failures=3
INFO  health: Proxy recovered proxy="mesh-jp" was_unhealthy_for="5m 23s"
```

### Failover Events
```
WARN  failover: Active proxy unhealthy, initiating failover from="mesh-us" reason="consecutive failures: 3"
INFO  failover: Failover complete from="mesh-us" to="mesh-eu" 
INFO  failover: Failback initiated to="mesh-us" reason="original proxy recovered"
WARN  failover: All proxies unhealthy, policy=use_last using="mesh-eu"
```

## File Changes Summary

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config.rs` | Add health check settings to Settings struct |
| `src/config.rs` | Add priority field to ProxyDef |
| `src/state.rs` | Add health fields to ProxyStats |
| `src/state.rs` | Add RuntimeState struct |
| `src/health.rs` | New module: health check logic |
| `src/failover.rs` | New module: failover/failback logic |
| `src/main.rs` | Spawn health check task in daemon |
| `src/main.rs` | Update status command output |
| `src/proxy.rs` | Use RuntimeState.effective_proxy |

## Testing Requirements

See dedicated subtask for comprehensive test suite.

## Rollout Strategy

1. **Phase 1: Health Checks Only**
   - Implement health check loop
   - Add status visibility
   - health_check_enabled = true, auto_failover = false
   - Users can see health but no automatic action

2. **Phase 2: Manual Failover**
   - Add `rust_proxy failover <proxy-id>` command
   - Users can manually trigger failover
   
3. **Phase 3: Auto-Failover**
   - Enable auto_failover = true by default
   - Full automatic operation

## Risk Assessment

- **Complexity**: High (new modules, state changes, daemon integration)
- **Impact**: Very high (major reliability improvement for production use)
- **Risk**: Medium (modifies core daemon behavior, needs careful testing)
- **Confidence**: High (well-defined patterns, clear success criteria, phased rollout)

## Mitigation Strategies

- Feature flag: health_check_enabled (can disable entirely)
- Phased rollout: health visibility before auto-failover
- Extensive logging for debugging
- Manual override: `rust_proxy activate --force` ignores health
- Conservative defaults: long intervals, high thresholds

## Dependencies

- **Requires**: Accept loop error recovery (rust_proxy-j41) for robust daemon
- **Enhances**: Status command with health information","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:49:15.780669041-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T10:49:42.936350577-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T10:49:42.936350577-05:00","close_reason":"All implementation subtasks complete: health check configuration (b5m.1), state management (b5m.2), health check loop (b5m.3), failover logic (b5m.4), failback logic (b5m.5), status visibility (b5m.6), RuntimeState (b5m.8). Test suite (b5m.7) remains open as follow-up.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_proxy-b5m","depends_on_id":"rust_proxy-j41","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:53:18.449147042-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
